title: Eriopappus paniculatus
intro: Eriopappus paniculatus, also known as White Sagebrush, is a perennial shrub native to the western United States and Canada, characterized by its silvery-gray foliage and white or pink flower clusters. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant effects, making it beneficial for relieving respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The bioactive constituents of Eriopappus paniculatus include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Eriopappus paniculatus is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, often combined with other herbs to enhance its therapeutic effects.
taxonomy: Eriopappus paniculatus is classified in the family Asteraceae, a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. The genus Eriopappus is a group of flowering plants native to North America, and Eriopappus paniculatus is one of the species within this genus. It is also known as panicled woollywhite or panicled cottonhead. Eriopappus paniculatus is a perennial herb that grows in the western United States and parts of Canada. This plant is often found in dry areas with rocky outcroppings and is a member of the Rosales order.
